title: Boiler Systems
summary: Covers auxiliary boiler operations, exhaust gas economizers, and boiler water chemistry management. Includes practical guidance on boiler water quality control, EGE soot blowing procedures, and wind box fire prevention — essential knowledge for 3AE candidates and watchkeeping engineers.
keyTopics: Exhaust Gas Economizer (EGE) and soot blowing
Wind box fire prevention and response

title: Diesel Generator
summary: Comprehensive diesel generator resource covering engine structure, AVR (Automatic Voltage Regulator) theory, governor operation, fuel filter maintenance, Pmax measurement, tappet clearance adjustment, and full safety device test procedures. Includes work instructions used onboard NYK vessels.
keyTopics: Safety device test procedures (full WI format)

title: Main Engine
summary: Main engine fundamentals including WinGD two-stroke diesel engine manual reference, piston removal procedures, cylinder lubrication rate theory, and MCR vs NOR concepts. Core knowledge for any marine engineer aspiring to Chief Engineer rank.
keyTopics: MCR (Maximum Continuous Rating) vs NOR (Normal Operating Rating)

title: Main Turbine (Steam Plant)
summary: Steam turbine plant operations for LNG and steam-powered vessels. Covers crash astern procedure, maneuvering valve operation, machine-side control, LP turbine blade design, and hydraulic bolt tensioning methods. Critical for engineers on steam-powered LNG carriers.

title: Turbo Generator
summary: Turbo Generator theory and practical operation guides. Covers TG instruction manuals, safety device test procedures, overspeed trip mechanism, SSS clutch operation, load limiting precautions, and why no-load operation should be avoided. Important for steam vessel engineers.

title: Emergency Generator
summary: Emergency generator load test procedures and safety device tests as required by SOLAS and classification societies. Includes dead ship recovery procedure — starting from total blackout back to normal power. A key topic in 3AE oral examinations.
keyTopics: EG load test procedure (start and restoration)
Dead ship recovery from total blackout
Load test flowchart and work instructions

title: Fuel Oil Purifier
summary: Complete purifier resource using Mitsubishi Kakoki (MKK) Selfjector H-Series as reference. Covers centrifugal separation theory, auto-operation sequences, time chart understanding, alarm monitoring, bowl disassembly and overhaul, and vertical/horizontal shaft maintenance. Includes training video reference.
keyTopics: Selfjector auto-operation sequence and time chart
Bowl disassembly and rotating assembly overhaul
Vertical shaft and horizontal shaft maintenance

title: Pumps
summary: Marine pump fundamentals including mechanical seal theory and maintenance, vacuum pump operation, trochoid pump principles, impeller orientation, and oil quench seal systems. Covers practical pump supplementary materials used for training junior engineers.

title: Electric Motors & Alignment
summary: Electric motor maintenance and shaft alignment (centering) procedures. Includes grease selection and application intervals, motor-inverter troubleshooting case studies, difference between rolling and sliding bearings, and motor control circuit fundamentals. Practical field-level knowledge from experienced 2nd Engineers.
keyTopics: Grease consistency (NLGI grade) and application intervals
Rolling bearing vs sliding bearing differences

title: Electrical Systems
summary: Essential electrical knowledge for marine engineers: insulation resistance testing and low insulation troubleshooting with a multimeter, why bow thrusters use 3300V high voltage systems, and the physics of reactive power. Foundation knowledge tested in 3rd Class licensing examinations.
keyTopics: Insulation resistance (IR) testing with a tester
Low insulation diagnosis and corrective action
High-voltage systems — why 3300V for bow thrusters

title: Governor Systems
summary: Governor theory and practical operation covering mechanical and electronic governors (Woodward). Explains droop control characteristics, isochronous vs droop mode, parallel generator operation, and load sharing. The droop concept is a frequent oral examination topic for 3AE/2AE candidates.
keyTopics: Load sharing in parallel generator operation

title: LSA & Fire Fighting Equipment
summary: Life Saving Appliances (LSA) and Fire Fighting Equipment (FFE) covering lifeboat launching, BA compressor testing, fixed foam system, CO2 system operation, fire hose pressure testing, fire detector testing (Autronica), high-expansion foam systems, dry chemical powder extinguishers, and SOFTEX water mist systems. SOLAS-mandatory knowledge.
keyTopics: Lifeboat launching drill and engine starting
Fire hose and reel pressure testing

title: Emergency Drills
summary: Emergency drill procedures including oil spill containment (SOPEP), damage control drills, CO2 release procedures, and bilge emergency discharge. Includes checklists and work instructions used on actual vessels. Understanding drill procedures is required by STCW and ISM Code.
keyTopics: Damage control drill procedures and checklist

title: Refrigeration & Air Conditioning
summary: Provision refrigeration and air conditioning systems covering refrigerant types (R404A, R449a), Mollier diagram interpretation, compressor overhaul procedures, refrigerant leak detection, refrigeration oil properties, and the regulatory transition to HFO-free refrigerants. Includes photo-documented compressor overhaul.
keyTopics: Refrigerant types — R404A, R449a, environmental regulations

title: Air Handling Unit (HVAC)
summary: Air Handling Unit maintenance covering evaporator cleaning procedures, cooler drain line maintenance, and alarm troubleshooting for accommodation and engine room HVAC systems. Includes package aircon alarm diagnosis.
keyTopics: Package aircon alarm types and response

title: LNG Vessel Operations
summary: LNG vessel-specific materials including drydock superintendent manuals, overseas stationed superintendent guides, fitting-out manuals for new LNG vessels, and class survey acceptance manuals. Essential reading for engineers targeting LNG carrier assignments.

title: Marine Engineer Licensing Exam
summary: Comprehensive licensing exam preparation materials for 3rd, 2nd, and 1st Class Marine Engineer (Kikan) certificates. Includes past oral examination questions from 2009–2024, topic-by-topic study guides (Diesel, Steam Turbine, Gas Turbine, Boiler, Aux Machinery, Electrical, Fuel, Automation, Maritime Law), and a practical exam questions bank.
keyTopics: Topic modules: Basic Knowledge, Diesel, Steam Turbine, Gas Turbine, Boiler, Auxiliary, Electrical, Fuel Oil, Automation, Maritime Law, Inspection

title: Control Engineering & PID
summary: Control engineering fundamentals applied to marine systems. Covers PID (Proportional-Integral-Derivative) control theory, feedback control loops, sensors including RTDs (resistance temperature detectors), flow meters (oval gear type), and practical automation system knowledge required for the 3AE licensing exam automation module.

title: Steering Gear
summary: Steering gear system fundamentals and emergency steering procedures. SOLAS-required knowledge for all watchkeeping officers. Covers hydraulic steering gear operation and emergency changeover to manual/local steering.
keyTopics: SOLAS requirements for steering gear drills

title: Valves & Piping
summary: Valve maintenance and piping systems covering gland packing replacement, accommodation hot water valve replacement, sea chest piping diagrams, Marine Growth Prevention System (MGPS) chloride testing and overhaul, and piping diagram interpretation.
keyTopics: Sea chest piping and sea water systems
MGPS (Marine Growth Prevention System) operation and maintenance
Accommodation hot water line valve replacement

title: Tools, Parts & Materials
summary: Practical knowledge on tools and spare parts used in engine room maintenance. Covers V-belt selection and tensioning, hydraulic jack operation for bolt tensioning, anti-seize compound application, and spare parts management concepts including a novel spare parts platform idea for the maritime industry.
keyTopics: Hydraulic jack operation for flange bolts
Anti-seize compound (焼き付き防止剤) usage

title: Fuel Oil Properties
summary: Fuel oil properties and management for marine diesel and steam plants. Covers HFO, VLSFO, and ULSFO characteristics, viscosity-temperature relationships, sulfur content regulations (IMO 2020), and fuel oil analysis and treatment.
keyTopics: VLSFO and ULSFO for IMO 2020 compliance

title: Maritime Conventions & Surveys
summary: Maritime conventions, statutory certificates, and survey requirements. Essential regulatory knowledge including SOLAS, MARPOL, STCW, Load Line Convention, and ISM Code. Covers what certificates a vessel must carry and the survey schedule for statutory inspections.
keyTopics: SOLAS — Safety of Life at Sea
sourceDir: 条約、証書等の規則関連

title: Switchboard & Power Distribution
summary: Main switchboard (MSB) and emergency switchboard (ESB) power distribution systems. Covers bus bar configuration, circuit breaker operation, power distribution topology on merchant vessels, and practical electrical panel maintenance.
keyTopics: Bus bar systems and power distribution

title: CAPAC (Cathodic Protection)
summary: Impressed Current Cathodic Protection (ICCP) system — CAPAC system documentation covering reference electrode assembly, main component functions, and operation manual. Prevents hull corrosion on steel vessels through controlled electric current.
